title: Auth Adapter Registry
description: Remote Studio may later require real credentials, but v1 ships with auth disabled on loopback. The registry keeps route handlers ignorant of verifier implementations: built-ins and plugins register by `type`, and composition resolves the active adapter from `specd.yaml` before the server accepts traffic.
context: Remote Studio may later require real credentials, but v1 ships with auth disabled on loopback. The registry keeps route handlers ignorant of verifier implementations: built-ins and plugins register by `type`, and composition resolves the active adapter from `specd.yaml` before the server accepts traffic.

rules.requirement: registry supports register and resolve by auth type
rules: The module MUST export `register(type: string, factory: AuthAdapterFactory)` and `resolve(type: string, config?: unknown): ApiTokenVerifier`. Each factory receives server bootstrap context including `ActorResolver`.

rules.requirement: default registry registers only disabled in v1
rules: `defaultAuthAdapterRegistry()` MUST register exactly one built-in type: `disabled` mapped to `adapter-auth-disabled`. Resolving any other type MUST throw before the HTTP server listens.

rules.requirement: createApiServer resolves verifier once at startup
rules: `createApiServer` MUST accept an optional injected registry (tests) or use the default, call `resolve(effectiveAuth.type, effectiveAuth.config)` once, and pass the verifier to `middleware-auth`.

constraints: @specd/api delivery and composition code MUST import host bootstrap and kernel types from @specd/sdk, not @specd/core or @specd/code-graph directly.
HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.requirement: registry supports register and resolve by auth type
scenarios.name: Register then resolve returns verifier

scenarios.requirement: registry supports register and resolve by auth type
scenarios.name: Unknown type throws at resolve
when: resolve("jwt") runs against v1 default registry
then: AND message identifies unknown auth type

scenarios.requirement: registry supports register and resolve by auth type
scenarios.name: Injected registry is used in tests
given: test passes custom registry with stub verifier
then: stub verifier is wired to middleware
AND default registry is not used

scenarios.requirement: default registry registers only disabled in v1
scenarios.name: Default registry lists only disabled
then: exactly one built-in type is registered

scenarios.requirement: default registry registers only disabled in v1
scenarios.name: Resolve disabled returns disabled adapter

scenarios.requirement: default registry registers only disabled in v1
scenarios.name: Resolving bearer fails in v1
when: resolve("bearer") is called on default registry
then: AND CLI --auth bearer is already rejected

scenarios.requirement: createApiServer resolves verifier once at startup
scenarios.name: Verifier is fixed for process lifetime
then: AND middleware does not re-resolve per request

scenarios.requirement: createApiServer resolves verifier once at startup
scenarios.name: Effective auth comes from specd.yaml merge
then: AND resolve uses that type once

scenarios.requirement: createApiServer resolves verifier once at startup
scenarios.name: Verifier passed to middleware-auth
then: AND handlers do not construct verifiers
